PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Renata Kiefer (Special Administrator).

Mr. Bridgman stated that it does not appear there is a safe deposit box, that the only assets are funds (about $535,000.00) left over from the sale of the house and that the funds were deposited with the Court through the civil matter (In Re: National Default Servicing Corporation CPF-18-516166) on August 14, 2018. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). Bond is not required at this time. Mr. Bridgman is to request that the funds go to the estate and will file an ex parte petition to either deposit the funds into a blocked account or to have bond issued. Status on close of estate is set for September 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-11-2019 Continued To Mar-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Naddafi appeared by telephone for Herman Holley (Petitioner).
James Bridgman for and with Renata Kiefer.

Mr. Nadaffi stated that Mr. Holley passed away in October, that he has been trying to find an heir to open probate for him and that at this point he does not have anyone to step in for him. Mr. Holley's Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is off calendar. Mr. Bridgman stated that they went to Ms. Heden's home to search for the will, and that there may be a safe deposit box, but they are not telling them. The Court appoints Renata Kiefer as the special administrator without bond for the purpose of searching for a will (subject to receipt and review of an order). The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Chan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-15-2018 Continued To Dec-10-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Bryan Naddafi for Herman Holley via Court Call.
James Bridgman for Heirs

Mr. Naddafi requested a 2-month continuance to locate a European heir search company with genealogist in Sweden. Mr. Bridgman stated he was prior counsel for Herman Holley, he has the names of the heirs if Mr. Naddafi wants them. Mr. Naddafi stated he is interested in working with Mr. Bridgman regarding heirs, and believe that the lost original Will is kept in a safety deposit box in a local Chase Bank. Mr. Bridgman confirmed that Chase Bank needed letters of administration before allowing access to the safety deposit box. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters of Testamentary is continued to December 10, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Oct-15-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bryan Naddafi appeared by telephone for Petitioner.

The Court stated that it does not have a declaration of due diligence indicating what anybody did to search for the will. Mr. Naddafi stated that there was a previous Attorney, that he believes this may have been addressed, that he will put together a declaration from his Client and get a hold of the other Attorney to sign the declaration. Mr. Naddafi also stated that he put the Decedent and the Decedent's Parents in the heir search, that they did not find anyone as of right now, that he doesn't believe there are any family or living heirs, and that he can do a skip trace. In addition, Mr. Naddafi stated that they do not require Independent Administration of Estates Act authority, and that he can strike that because they do not have to sell or liquidate assets. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to October 15, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court stated that this is on for petition to probate a will as well as a petition for proof of lost will and that Mr. Bridgman was to be looking for the original will but is not here to report. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition of Herman Holley for Proof of Lost Will were continued to October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and the Court stated that Mr. Bridgman should have taken care of the continuance sooner than yesterday.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not reported)

Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Continued To Sep-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman, for petitioner Herman Holley.

Mr. Bridgman informed the Court that he had one further avenue to explore in his search for the missing will. He said that he had found documentation for a safety deposit box that he wanted to pursue, and requested that the matter be further continued for one month. The matter was continued to September 27, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Off Calendar Aug-02-2016 Continued To Aug-30-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jim Bridgeman for petitioner Herman Holley

Petition Of Herman Holley For Proof Of Lost Will Pursuant To Ccp 8223
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-05-2016 Continued To Aug-02-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Herman Holley.

The Court stated that this is a petition to probate a lost will, that there is only a copy, that the original has not been found, that the attorney believes that if the decedent wanted to change or destroy the will he would have been contacted and that this is slim evidence. Mr. Bridgman stated that he knew the decedent well, that declarations have been submitted, that Mr. Holley lived with the decedent for 40 years and that he paid the second on the house. The Court stated that it has a statute to deal with. Mr. Bridgman stated that Mr. Heden was taciturn, that he would not have talked about the will and that if the Court is not going to grant the petition would like more time to find additional evidence. The Court stated that if there is more evidence it would be helpful and that it is the statutory presumption that the Court needs to deal with. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary and the Petition of Herman Holley for Proof of Lost Will were continued to August 2, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for additional evidence.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)
